name: Christmas Tree Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F).
2. Grease two baking sheets.
3. Sift together the plain flour, bicarbonate of soda, and mixed spice into a bowl.
4. Rub the chilled butter into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs.
5. Stir in the brown sugar.
6. Add the heated golden syrup and beaten egg, then mix to form a dough.
7. Gently knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth.
8. Roll out the dough to 1/4 inch thick. Use Christmas cutters to cut out 16 shapes.
9. Lift the shaped biscuits onto the prepared baking sheets.
10. Bake until just firm to the touch. Make a hole large enough for gold thread at the top of each biscuit.
11. Transfer to a cooling rack and leave until cold.
12. To decorate, roll out the fondant icing on a clean surface dusted with icing sugar.
13. Cut out shapes with cutters and place them on the biscuits, securing them with a little water.
14. Make a hole for thread as before.
15. Decorate with piped glace icing, silver balls, sweets, or coconut.
16. Thread each biscuit with gold thread and hang on the Christmas tree, if desired.
